revision.comment: Grundinfos
revision.text:
{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = F27-F27-T19-T19 N14 N33*N33:N33:N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Septi-chenwi
''{{unicode|Sp.tj-ḥn.wj}}''
''Die beiden Fische'' }} '''Septi-chenwi''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]], der vier [[Baktiu|Dekan-Sterne]] umfasste. Die auffälligsten [[Stern]]e dieses Dekans sind [[Kaus Medius]] im [[Sternbild]] [[Schütze (Sternbild)|Schütze]] sowie [[Yed Prior]] und [[Yed Posterior]] im Sternbild [[Schlangenträger]]. In den Dekanlisten am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] nahm Septi-chenwi in der Sethos-Schrift den 14. Platz ein; in der Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] wurde Septi-chenwi als 13. Dekan geführt, dessen [[heliakisch]]er Aufgang für den [[Achet#Datierungen|6. Achet I]] angesetzt war. == Siehe auch == * [[Nutbuch]] == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 90. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 62–67 und S. 385. [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = F27-F27-T19-T19 N14 N33*N33:N33:N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Septi-chenwi
''Sp.tj-ḥn.wj''
''The Two Fishes'' }} '''Septi-chenwi''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}}s of this decan are {{ill|Delta Sagittarii|de|Delta Sagittarii}} (Kaus Medius) in the {{ill|Sagittarius (constellation)|de|Schütze (Sternbild)}} constellation, as well as {{ill|Delta Ophiuchi|de|Delta Ophiuchi}} and {{ill|Epsilon Ophiuchi|de|Epsilon Ophiuchi}} (Yed Prior and Yed Posterior) in the constellation {{ill|Ophiuchus|de|Schlangenträger}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Septi-chenwi represented the 13th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet I|de|6. Achet I}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 90. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[ja:セプティ・チェンウィ]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[de:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = F27-F27-T19-T19 N14 N33*N33:N33:N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Septi-chenwi
''Sp.tj-ḥn.wj''
''The Two Fishes'' }} '''Septi-chenwi''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}}s of this decan are {{ill|Delta Sagittarii|de|Delta Sagittarii}} (Kaus Medius) in the {{ill|Sagittarius (constellation)|de|Schütze (Sternbild)}} constellation, as well as {{ill|Delta Ophiuchi|de|Delta Ophiuchi}} and {{ill|Epsilon Ophiuchi|de|Epsilon Ophiuchi}} (Yed Prior and Yed Posterior) in the constellation {{ill|Ophiuchus|de|Schlangenträger}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Septi-chenwi represented the 13th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet I|de|6. Achet I}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 90. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[ja:セプティ・チェンウィ]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:new pages]][[en:draft:Septi-chenwi]] [[de:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:new pages]]

In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Septi-chenwi represented the 13th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. Achet I}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = F27-F27-T19-T19 N14 N33*N33:N33:N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Septi-chenwi
''Sp.tj-ḥn.wj''
''The Two Fishes'' }} '''Septi-chenwi''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}}s of this decan are {{ill|Delta Sagittarii|de|Delta Sagittarii}} (Kaus Medius) in the {{ill|Sagittarius (constellation)|de|Schütze (Sternbild)}} constellation, as well as {{ill|Delta Ophiuchi|de|Delta Ophiuchi}} and {{ill|Epsilon Ophiuchi|de|Epsilon Ophiuchi}} (Yed Prior and Yed Posterior) in the constellation {{ill|Ophiuchus|de|Schlangenträger}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Septi-chenwi represented the 13th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet I|de|6. Achet I}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 90. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = F27-F27-T19-T19 N14 N33*N33:N33:N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Septi-chenwi
''Sp.tj-ḥn.wj''
''The Two Fishes'' }} '''Septi-chenwi''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}}s of this decan are {{ill|Delta Sagittarii|de|Delta Sagittarii}} (Kaus Medius) in the {{ill|Sagittarius (constellation)|de|Schütze (Sternbild)}} constellation, as well as {{ill|Delta Ophiuchi|de|Delta Ophiuchi}} and {{ill|Epsilon Ophiuchi|de|Epsilon Ophiuchi}} (Yed Prior and Yed Posterior) in the constellation {{ill|Ophiuchus|de|Schlangenträger}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Septi-chenwi represented the 13th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet I|de|6. Achet I}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 90. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[ja:セプティ・チェンウィ]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Septi-chenwi]][[en:draft:Septi-chenwi]] [[de:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1 pages]] [[Category:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Septi-chenwi]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:11]]

''Sp.tj-ḥn.wj''
''The Two Fishes'' }} '''Septi-chenwi''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}}s of this decan are {{ill|Delta Sagittarii|de|Delta Sagittarii}} (Kaus Medius) in the {{ill|Sagittarius (constellation)|de|Schütze (Sternbild)}} constellation, as well as {{ill|Delta Ophiuchi|de|Delta Ophiuchi}} and {{ill|Epsilon Ophiuchi|de|Epsilon Ophiuchi}} (Yed Prior and Yed Posterior) in the constellation {{ill|Ophiuchus|de|Schlangenträger}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Septi-chenwi represented the 13th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet I|de|6. Achet I}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 90. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[ja:セプティ・チェンウィ]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Septi-chenwi]][[en:draft:Septi-chenwi]] [[de:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:Septi-chenwi]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[sn:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]]

The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}}s of this decan are {{ill|Delta Sagittarii|de|Delta Sagittarii}} (Kaus Medius) in the {{ill|Sagittarius (constellation)|de|Schütze (Sternbild)}} constellation, as well as {{ill|Delta Ophiuchi|de|Delta Ophiuchi}} and {{ill|Epsilon Ophiuchi|de|Epsilon Ophiuchi}} (Yed Prior and Yed Posterior) in the constellation {{ill|Ophiuchus|de|Schlangenträger}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Septi-chenwi represented the 13th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet I|de|6. Achet I}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 90. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = F27-F27-T19-T19 N14 N33*N33:N33:N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Septi-chenwi
''Sp.tj-ḥn.wj''
''The Two Fishes'' }} '''Septi-chenwi''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|12=draft|12=draft}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|12=draft|12=draft}} (decan stars). The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern|12=draft|12=draft}}s of this decan are {{ill|Delta Sagittarii|de|Delta Sagittarii|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kaus Medius) in the {{ill|Sagittarius (constellation)|de|Schütze (Sternbild)|12=draft|12=draft}} constellation, as well as {{ill|Delta Ophiuchi|de|Delta Ophiuchi|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Epsilon Ophiuchi|de|Epsilon Ophiuchi|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yed Prior and Yed Posterior) in the constellation {{ill|Ophiuchus|de|Schlangenträger|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=draft|12=draft}}, Septi-chenwi represented the 13th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|12=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|12=draft|12=draft}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet I|de|6. Achet I|12=draft|12=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|12=draft|12=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 90. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{draft categories| [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Septi-chenwi]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic ]] [[Category: in namespace ]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:セプティ・チェンウィ]] [[en:draft:Septi-chenwi]] [[de:Septi-chenwi]] [[sn:Septi-chenwi]]

Achet I|qq=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|qq=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 90. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern|qq=draft}}s of this decan are {{ill|Delta Sagittarii|de|Delta Sagittarii|qq=draft}} (Kaus Medius) in the {{ill|Sagittarius (constellation)|de|Schütze (Sternbild)|qq=draft}} constellation, as well as {{ill|Delta Ophiuchi|de|Delta Ophiuchi|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Epsilon Ophiuchi|de|Epsilon Ophiuchi|qq=draft}} (Yed Prior and Yed Posterior) in the constellation {{ill|Ophiuchus|de|Schlangenträger|qq=draft}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft}}, Septi-chenwi represented the 13th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|qq=draft}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet I|de|6. Achet I|qq=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|qq=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 90. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{translated page|de|Septi-chenwi|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Septi-chenwi]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic ]] }} [[sn:Septi-chenwi]] [[ja:セプティ・チェンウィ]] [[en:draft:Septi-chenwi]] [[de:Septi-chenwi]] [[sn: failed]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = F27-F27-T19-T19 N14 N33*N33:N33:N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Septi-chenwi
''Sp.tj-ḥn.wj''
''The Two Fishes'' }} '''Septi-chenwi''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan stars). The most prominent [[:en:Star|Star]]s of this decan are {{ill|Delta Sagittarii|de|Delta Sagittarii|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Delta Sagittarii|qq=}} (Kaus Medius) in the [[:en:Sagittarius (constellation)|Sagittarius (constellation)]] constellation, as well as [[:en:Delta Ophiuchi|Delta Ophiuchi]] and [[:en:Epsilon Ophiuchi|Epsilon Ophiuchi]] (Yed Prior and Yed Posterior) in the constellation [[:en:Ophiuchus|Ophiuchus]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Septi-chenwi represented the 13th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet I|de|6. Achet I|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 90. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{translated page|de|Septi-chenwi|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic ]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Septi-chenwi]] [[ja:セプティ・チェンウィ]] [[en:draft:Septi-chenwi]] [[de:Septi-chenwi]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 90. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. revision.comment: Bot: prepend Wikidata AFC comment via pages.txt
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = F27-F27-T19-T19 N14 N33*N33:N33:N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Septi-chenwi
''Sp.tj-ḥn.wj''
''The Two Fishes'' }} '''Septi-chenwi''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan stars). The most prominent [[:en:Star|Star]]s of this decan are {{ill|Delta Sagittarii|de|Delta Sagittarii|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Delta Sagittarii|qq=}} (Kaus Medius) in the [[:en:Sagittarius (constellation)|Sagittarius (constellation)]] constellation, as well as [[:en:Delta Ophiuchi|Delta Ophiuchi]] and [[:en:Epsilon Ophiuchi|Epsilon Ophiuchi]] (Yed Prior and Yed Posterior) in the constellation [[:en:Ophiuchus|Ophiuchus]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Septi-chenwi represented the 13th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet I|de|6. Achet I|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 90. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{translated page|de|Septi-chenwi|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Septi-chenwi]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Septi-chenwi]] }} [[sn:Septi-chenwi]] [[ja:セプティ・チェンウィ]] [[de:Septi-chenwi]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = F27-F27-T19-T19 N14 N33*N33:N33:N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Septi-chenwi
''Sp.tj-ḥn.wj''
''The Two Fishes'' }} '''Septi-chenwi''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan stars). The most prominent [[:en:Star|Star]]s of this decan are {{ill|Delta Sagittarii|de|Delta Sagittarii|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Delta Sagittarii|qq=}} (Kaus Medius) in the [[:en:Sagittarius (constellation)|Sagittarius (constellation)]] constellation, as well as [[:en:Delta Ophiuchi|Delta Ophiuchi]] and [[:en:Epsilon Ophiuchi|Epsilon Ophiuchi]] (Yed Prior and Yed Posterior) in the constellation [[:en:Ophiuchus|Ophiuchus]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Septi-chenwi represented the 13th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet I|de|6. Achet I|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 90. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{translated page|de|Septi-chenwi|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Septi-chenwi]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Septi-chenwi]] }} [[sn:Septi-chenwi]] [[ja:セプティ・チェンウィ]] [[de:Septi-chenwi]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Wikidata entry [[d:" to "Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q2271782]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:26, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = F27-F27-T19-T19 N14 N33*N33:N33:N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Septi-chenwi
''Sp.tj-ḥn.wj''
''The Two Fishes'' }} '''Septi-chenwi''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan stars). The most prominent [[:en:Star|Star]]s of this decan are {{ill|Delta Sagittarii|de|Delta Sagittarii|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Delta Sagittarii|qq=}} (Kaus Medius) in the [[:en:Sagittarius (constellation)|Sagittarius (constellation)]] constellation, as well as [[:en:Delta Ophiuchi|Delta Ophiuchi]] and [[:en:Epsilon Ophiuchi|Epsilon Ophiuchi]] (Yed Prior and Yed Posterior) in the constellation [[:en:Ophiuchus|Ophiuchus]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Septi-chenwi represented the 13th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet I|de|6. Achet I|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 90. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{translated page|de|Septi-chenwi|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Septi-chenwi]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Septi-chenwi]] }} [[sn:Septi-chenwi]] [[ja:セプティ・チェンウィ]] [[de:Septi-chenwi]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 90. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{translated page|de|Septi-chenwi|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Septi-chenwi]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Septi-chenwi]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Septi-chenwi]] }} [[sn:Septi-chenwi]] [[ja:セプティ・チェンウィ]] [[de:Septi-chenwi]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

[[de:Septi-chenwi]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q2271782]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:26, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = F27-F27-T19-T19 N14 N33*N33:N33:N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Septi-chenwi
''Sp.tj-ḥn.wj''
''The Two Fishes'' }} '''Septi-chenwi''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} (decan stars). The most prominent [[:en:Star|Star]]s of this decan are {{ill|Delta Sagittarii|de|Delta Sagittarii|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Delta Sagittarii|qq=|WD=Q14038}} (Kaus Medius) in the [[:en:Sagittarius (constellation)|Sagittarius]] constellation, as well as [[:en:Delta Ophiuchi|Delta Ophiuchi]] and [[:en:Epsilon Ophiuchi|Epsilon Ophiuchi]] (Yed Prior and Yed Posterior) in the constellation [[:en:Ophiuchus|Ophiuchus]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}}, Septi-chenwi represented the 13th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet I|de|6. Achet I|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 90. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{translated page|de|Septi-chenwi|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:セプティ・チェンウィ]] [[de:Septi-chenwi]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] {{wikidata link|Q2271782}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

''The Two Fishes'' }} '''Septi-chenwi''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} (decan stars). The most prominent [[:en:Star|Star]]s of this decan are {{ill|Delta Sagittarii|de|Delta Sagittarii|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Delta Sagittarii|qq=|WD=Q14038}} (Kaus Medius) in the [[:en:Sagittarius (constellation)|Sagittarius]] constellation, as well as [[:en:Delta Ophiuchi|Delta Ophiuchi]] and [[:en:Epsilon Ophiuchi|Epsilon Ophiuchi]] (Yed Prior and Yed Posterior) in the constellation [[:en:Ophiuchus|Ophiuchus]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}}, Septi-chenwi represented the 13th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet I|de|6. Achet I|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 90. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{translated page|de|Septi-chenwi|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:セプティ・チェンウィ]] [[de:Septi-chenwi]] {{wikidata link|Q2271782}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}}, Septi-chenwi represented the 13th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|en|Heliacal rising|WD=Q368373|lt=Heliacal rising}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet I|de|6. Achet I|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|WD=Q19248|lt=Senusret III}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q719639|lt=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 90. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{translated page|de|Septi-chenwi|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[ja:セプティ・チェンウィ]] [[de:Septi-chenwi]] {{wikidata link|Q2271782}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]
